meaning: vt.使骨化,使硬化vi.骨化,硬化,变得无情
examples: Raymond: that it is possible for open source cultures in some respects to ossify enough that good work is locked out.
But the risk that the recent homeward flow of bank assets will ossify into a less efficient, less flexible cross-border banking system is real and growing.
"Because economies that work only for the few and do not bring out the best in all its people will ossify and be left behind, equality of opportunity is an economic necessity, " he said.
